meta_title: Roofing in Tamarac, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Tamarac, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, A+ BBB, financing from $87/mo. Free estimates.
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair for Tamarac Homeowners
intro:
Tamarac is a city that takes pride in its well-established neighborhoods — from the tree-lined streets of Woodlands and the golf-course community of Colony West to the quiet cul-de-sacs of Mainlands and Tamarac Lakes. With average home values sitting around $300,000, your home represents one of the most significant investments you'll ever make. And in South Florida, that investment is only as secure as the roof above it. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in providing Tamarac homeowners with durable, code-compliant, hurricane-rated roofing systems that stand up to everything Broward County's climate can throw at them.

With approximately 55% of Tamarac homes built before 1994 — many pre-dating the sweeping changes brought on by Hurricane Andrew — a large portion of roofs in this city are aging past their serviceable life expectancy. Tile systems that were installed in the late 1980s and shingle roofs from the 1990s weren't designed to meet today's Florida Building Code standards, and many have already been patched, re-nailed, or partially repaired multiple times. If your home sits in ZIP codes 33309, 33319, 33320, 33321, or 33351, there's a strong chance your roof is due for a full evaluation — and possibly a full replacement.

Tamarac may sit approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coast, but don't let that distance lull you into a false sense of security. Broward County is classified as a 170 mph wind zone, meaning every roofing system installed here must be engineered and rated to withstand Category 4 equivalent wind forces. Hurricane Wilma in 2005 and Hurricane Irma in 2017 caused widespread roof damage across Tamarac, and the city has experienced no fewer than 9 named storms since 1990. The question for most homeowners isn't whether their roof will be tested — it's whether it will pass. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing gives you a roofing system built to do exactly that.

section_why.h2: Why Tamarac Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of roofing contractors operating in Broward County, but very few can match the combination of experience, accountability, and craftsmanship that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings to every project. With over 60 years of combined installation experience across our in-house team and more than 500 completed homes throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we've built a reputation as The Kings of Service — not just in name, but in practice. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not handing your roof off to a rotating cast of subcontractors. Every installation is completed by our own licensed and insured crew, giving us full control over quality at every step.

This matters enormously in Tamarac, where older home construction means our crews often encounter legacy roofing systems with decades of wear, improper layering, or structural decking that needs attention before a new system goes on. Our team knows how to assess and address these issues properly — not just cover them up. Whether you live near Colony West Country Club and have a clay tile roof approaching 30 years old, or you're in a Tamarac Lakes home with a 3-tab shingle system that's past its prime, we tailor every project to the specific conditions and needs of your home.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we pull every permit required through the City of Tamarac Building Division. We don't cut corners, we don't skip inspections, and we don't disappear after the job is done. Our post-installation support and warranty coverage give homeowners long-term peace of mind that fly-by-night contractors simply can't provide. When your home is your most valuable asset, you deserve a roofing partner who treats it that way.

We also understand the economic realities of Tamarac's lower-middle income community. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting from just $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for homeowners across every ZIP code in Tamarac to access top-tier roofing without financial hardship. A new roof doesn't have to break the bank when you work with a team that's genuinely invested in your community.

section_history.h2: Tamarac's Storm History Makes a Strong Roof Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Tamarac has lived through some of South Florida's most destructive weather events. Hurricane Andrew in 1992, while hitting hardest in Miami-Dade, sent damaging wind bands through Broward County that exposed how poorly many older homes were built to handle tropical systems. That storm fundamentally changed Florida's building codes, but homes already constructed were grandfathered in — and many of those same roofs are still in place in Tamarac today. Thirteen years later, Hurricane Wilma made landfall in October 2005 as a Category 3 storm and became one of the costliest weather events in Broward County history, ripping off tiles, peeling back shingles, and exposing thousands of homes to interior water damage that took months to repair.

Hurricane Irma followed in 2017, and while it weakened before reaching Broward County, sustained tropical storm to Category 1 winds across the area caused significant roof damage — particularly to older homes in neighborhoods like Woodlands and Mainlands where roofing systems hadn't been replaced since the pre-Andrew era. Neighborhoods near open areas, like those adjacent to Tamarac Park and the Woodlands golf corridor, experienced elevated wind exposure due to reduced natural wind barriers. Since 1990, Tamarac has absorbed the effects of at least 9 named storms, a pattern that shows no sign of slowing as Atlantic hurricane seasons grow more active.

The lesson from each of these storms is consistent: a properly installed, hurricane-rated roofing system is the single most important layer of protection your home has. Water intrusion through a compromised roof leads to mold, structural decay, and interior damage that far exceeds the cost of a proactive replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps Tamarac homeowners get ahead of the next storm — not catch up after it.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Tamarac and Broward County
section_code.body:
Tamarac falls under the jurisdiction of Broward County and is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), which mandates specific standards for all roofing systems based on the local wind design speed. Tamarac sits in a 170 mph wind zone, which means every new roofing system must be designed, rated, and installed to withstand those forces. While Tamarac is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved for Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities — the city still requires hurricane-rated products and adherence to FBC Chapter 15 roofing provisions. This means product approvals, installation methods, and fastener schedules all carry significant regulatory weight.

For shingle roofing, the Florida Building Code requires specific nailing patterns, underlayment specifications, and product approval numbers that confirm the system has been tested to meet wind uplift resistance standards. For tile roofing, products must meet FL Product Approval requirements and be installed with proper adhesive or mechanical fastening systems capable of resisting 170 mph wind loads. Cutting corners on any of these requirements doesn't just risk a failed inspection — it can void your insurance coverage and leave your home vulnerable to catastrophic damage.

All roofing work in Tamarac requires a permit pulled through the City of Tamarac Building Division, followed by inspections at key stages of the project. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process on your behalf — from permit application to final inspection sign-off. We submit all required FL Product Approval documentation, coordinate inspection scheduling, and ensure your project passes every stage of review. Homeowners who hire unlicensed contractors or attempt to skip the permit process face serious legal and financial exposure, especially when filing insurance claims after a storm. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, compliance is never an afterthought — it's built into every project from day one.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles and West Lake Tile: The Right Roofing Systems for Tamarac
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing lines that are ideally suited to the climate, aesthetic, and structural demands of Tamarac homes: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Together, these brands cover the full range of roofing styles found across Tamarac's neighborhoods, from the ranch-style homes in Mainlands to the Mediterranean-influenced designs near Colony West Country Club.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles are among the most respected in the industry, offering a significant step up from basic 3-tab shingles in both wind resistance and visual depth. The Landmark Series carries a Class A fire rating and is engineered to resist wind speeds up to 110 mph at standard installation — but with enhanced nailing schedules required under Florida Building Code, these systems are installed to perform at significantly higher thresholds. The dimensional profile of architectural shingles adds genuine curb appeal while also providing better water-shedding performance than flat-tab designs. For Tamarac homeowners on a budget or whose HOA specifications call for shingle roofing, CertainTeed Landmark offers outstanding long-term value.

For homes with existing tile roofs or homeowners seeking the premium look and longevity of tile, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs West Lake concrete and clay tile systems, which are specifically manufactured and approved for use in Florida's demanding climate. West Lake tile carries the Florida Product Approvals necessary for Broward County's wind zone requirements and is available in a wide range of profiles and colors to match or enhance your home's existing character. Concrete tile typically carries a 50-year warranty expectation, while clay tile can last even longer with proper maintenance. Both options significantly outperform asphalt shingles in terms of wind uplift resistance when installed with the proper mechanical fastening systems our crews use on every project.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Tamarac, FL — and How Do You Pay for It?
section_cost.body:
Roof replacement costs in Tamarac vary based on roof size, pitch, material selection, and the condition of the existing decking. For a typical Tamarac home — which averages around 1,500 to 2,200 square feet of living space — a full shingle replacement using CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingles generally ranges from $12,000 to $20,000. A tile roof replacement using West Lake concrete or clay tile systems typically ranges from $18,000 to $35,000 or more depending on tile style and roof complexity. While these numbers may seem significant, they need to be weighed against both the risk of storm damage from an aging roof and the immediate financial return through insurance savings.

For homeowners in Tamarac's lower-middle income community, the upfront cost of a roof replacement can feel overwhelming — and that's exactly why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing solutions designed to make roofing accessible. With options starting at just $87 per month and $0 down payment required, qualified homeowners can begin the project immediately and spread the investment over time. We work with lending partners who understand the South Florida market and can accommodate a range of credit profiles. The bottom line is that your family's safety and your home's structural integrity shouldn't be delayed because of financing concerns.

From a pure return-on-investment standpoint, a new roof on a $300,000 Tamarac home consistently returns strong value. Real estate professionals in Broward County regularly report that updated, code-compliant roofs increase marketability and appraisal values — and in some cases, a new roof is the deciding factor for buyers seeking homes in flood and wind-prone ZIP codes. Add the insurance premium savings that come with a new wind-mitigation-certified roof (often $1,500 to $3,500 per year), and many Tamarac homeowners find that a new roof pays for itself faster than they expected.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Benefits and Wind Mitigation Credits for Tamarac Roof Replacements
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial reasons to replace your roof in Tamarac is the direct impact it has on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida insurers are required to offer discounts based on a home's wind mitigation features, and your roof is the single most influential factor in that calculation. After a qualifying roof replacement, homeowners are eligible to have a Wind Mitigation Inspection performed by a licensed inspector, who documents features like roof covering type, roof deck attachment method, roof-to-wall connections, and roof shape. The results of this inspection are submitted to your insurance carrier, often resulting in annual premium reductions of $1,500 to $3,500 — sometimes more for larger homes.

Broward County homeowners have been hit particularly hard by the Florida insurance market crisis, with many carriers either pulling out of the state entirely or dramatically raising premiums. A new, code-compliant roof installed by a licensed contractor — with all permits properly pulled and inspections passed — can be the difference between keeping your current insurer, qualifying for a new policy, or receiving the maximum available wind mitigation discount under Citizens Property Insurance or private carriers like Universal, Heritage, or Slide. Insurers heavily scrutinize roofs older than 15 to 20 years, and some will not renew policies on homes with aging roofs regardless of claim history.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes the post-installation process as smooth as possible. After your new roof is installed and the final inspection is passed, we provide all necessary documentation — including product approval numbers, installation records, and permit closure confirmation — that your inspector and insurance carrier will need to process your wind mitigation credit application. Our customers in Tamarac have reported significant, often immediate premium reductions after completing their roof replacement with our team, transforming what felt like a major expense into a long-term financial win.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Tamarac home needs a full roof replacement versus a repair?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 15 to 20 years old, has experienced multiple storms including Wilma or Irma, or shows signs of widespread granule loss, cracked or missing tiles, or recurring leaks in multiple areas, a full replacement is almost always the more cost-effective and code-compliant solution. Patch repairs on aging systems often provide only temporary relief and may not meet current Florida Building Code standards required by your insurer. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free roof assessments to help Tamarac homeowners make informed decisions.

faqs.q: What does roof financing look like for Tamarac homeowners?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down payment options for qualified applicants. We work with lending partners familiar with South Florida homeowners and can accommodate various credit situations. Financing approval is typically fast, allowing your project to begin without delay. Given the insurance savings a new roof can generate — often $1,500 to $3,500 annually — many homeowners find their monthly financing payment is partially or fully offset by reduced insurance premiums.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for a roof replacement in Tamarac, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Tamarac require a permit through the City of Tamarac Building Division, along with inspections at key project milestones. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission and FL Product Approval documentation to inspection coordination and final sign-off. Homeowners who skip permits risk insurance claim denials, legal liability, and complications when selling their home. Every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing project is fully permitted and code-compliant.

faqs.q: How long does a full roof replacement take in Tamarac?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements in Tamarac are completed within one to three days for shingle systems, depending on roof size and complexity. Tile roof replacements typically take three to five days or more. The permitting process, which Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles in advance, generally adds one to two weeks to the overall project timeline. We schedule projects efficiently and keep homeowners informed at every stage. Our in-house crews — not subcontractors — complete all work, which keeps timelines predictable and quality consistent.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Tamarac?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After your new roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector documents its features, and those findings are submitted to your insurer. Tamarac homeowners with updated, code-compliant roofs regularly see annual premium reductions of $1,500 to $3,500 or more through wind mitigation credits. Additionally, many insurance carriers in Broward County will not renew or write new policies on homes with roofs older than 20 years, making a new roof essential for maintaining insurable status in today's Florida market.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Tamarac Home With a New Roof? Let's Talk.
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to assess your Tamarac roof, walk you through your options, and provide a free, no-pressure estimate — with financing starting at $87/mo and $0 down to make it easy to get started today.
